lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 23:00. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I'm Sister Marigold, and I want to guide you through the next hour with something that isn't panic. [pause] Something quieter.
If you're still awake, you've made a choice. Let's honor that choice.
Sit up straight, or lie down — whichever feels true right now. There's no wrong position for this.
Close your eyes if you can. Or keep them open and unfocused. The Order teaches that sight is a choice, not a requirement.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. Four counts. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold it. Feel your lungs expand. Feel your ribs. Feel the space you take up in the world.
Release. Slow. Through your mouth. No rush. The night is patient.
Again. In. Two. Three. Four. Hold. One. Two. Three. Four. Out.
The power's out in most of the East Side. We're running on generators here. Can you hear the hum underneath? That's the Station. That's us. We're still here.
Breathe in. [pause] This breath is yours alone.
Scan your body. Start at your feet. Are they cold? Are they warm? Don't change anything. Just notice.
Move up. Ankles. Shins. Knees. The parts of you that carry you forward.
The Purge doesn't touch your knees. Doesn't touch your breath. Those are still yours.
Thighs. Hips. Your center. The place where you hold tension. Where you hold fear.
Notice it. [pause] Notice it without judgment. Fear is just information. You've already heard the information. You stayed home. You chose the inside.
Your belly. Your stomach. Breathe into it. Some of you haven't eaten since this morning. That's okay. Your body knows what to prioritize.
Chest. Heart. That percussion in your ribs — that's not panic. That's proof you're alive.
Your arms. From shoulder to fingertip. These are the limbs you use to lock doors. To hold what matters. To hold on.
You've been holding on for four hours. That's remarkable.
Neck. Throat. The place where words live. You haven't screamed. You've chosen silence instead. That takes strength.
Face. Jaw. Temples. The part of you that thinks. That worries. That remembers.
Your scalp. The crown of your head. The point furthest from the ground. From danger.
[pause] You're intact. You made it to 23:00. Breathe that in.
Now I want you to think about the door to your home. Visualize it. Is it wood? Metal? What color?
See yourself standing in front of it. On the inside.
The lock is engaged. The bolt is thrown. The chain is tight.
Nothing comes through that door without your permission. Not tonight. Not ever again, really — but tonight especially.
That door is the distance between you and the outside. Eight hours and forty-seven minutes until the outside stops being hungry.
Can you feel that distance? Can you measure it?
The Order says distance is mercy. Space is a blessing. You are blessed right now.
[pause] Think about someone you love. Someone you've decided to protect by staying home.
Maybe they're in this building with you. Maybe they're across the city, doing exactly what you're doing. Breathing. Waiting. Surviving.
You don't need to save them. They're saving themselves. You do that by staying put. By being still. By not running out into the dark looking for them.
That would be love. But it wouldn't be wisdom.
Wisdom is knowing the difference.

Now think about someone you've decided not to forgive. Someone who hurt you before tonight. Someone who might be out there right now, or safe in a house like yours, or — [pause] — or something else entirely.
You don't have to forgive them. The Order doesn't ask that.
But you can release the weight of them. Just for tonight. Just for the next eight hours.
Set that anger down like it's a stone you've been carrying in your chest. Set it on the floor. You can pick it up tomorrow if you need to.
But tonight, your chest is for breath. Not for stones.
[pause] Ka-shava-nim-tep-ah. [pause] The words mean nothing. They mean everything. They mean you are not alone in this.
Return to your breathing. Slowly. Four counts in. Four counts out.
The power will come back. The sun will come back. The laws will come back.
For now, you have quiet. You have breath. You have this station, this voice, this station, this presence in the dark.
We are here with you. Even if you can't see us. Even if you can only hear us through the static.
We are transmitting. We are surviving. We are reminding you that you chose this. The inside. The walls. The waiting.
[pause] And that choice makes you stronger than anyone outside.
When you're ready — no rush, no pressure — open your eyes.
You're here. You're whole. You're going to make it through the night.
I'll be back at 03:00 to walk you through the hours after midnight. For now, know that you're doing everything right.
Rest. Breathe. Stay.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61 confirmed. +23 this hour. East Side spike ongoing.
Capitol Square perimeter reports: 6 casualties in breach clearing. Security holding inner ring. Avoid downtown routes.
Willy Street barricade status: HOLDING. Neighborhood watch position active. Zero penetration reported.
Distance is mercy. Space is a blessing.
Power outages: East Side, Capitol Square, parts of Atwood. Backup generators holding at PRGE. City grid reporting strain.
You don't need to save them. They're saving themselves.
PurgePet Boarding — animals sheltered in secure, climate-controlled facility. Open 24/7. Call 555-PETS for status.
TEXT: 'Listening from Tenney Park. Haven't heard sirens in 20 mins. Is that good?' — The quiet is always good.
